File Tree Explorer —
Toolskin · Component

File Tree Explorer

A token-driven tree component for navigating any hierarchy — file systems, tables of contents, sitemaps, settings outlines. Every gap, padding, border, surface and connector is a derivative of a single base token, so the whole component rescales and re-themes from one knob.

  • Bulletproof connectors — straight or curved, geometry-driven, never break.
  • Three states — hover, active (keyboard cursor), selected — each visually distinct.
  • Twin badges + folder-open swap — folder rows reveal folder / file counts; the glyph flips when open.
  • Theme-free — light / dark and accent re-tinting are owned by the Toolskin runtime.
usage.html 
<!-- 1. Drop in a placeholder — this is the ONLY markup you author. -->
<div id="my-tree" class="ts-tree"></div>

<!-- 2. Feed it data (filesystem, sitemap, TOC, JSON, anything). -->
<script>
ToolskinTree.init({
  target: '#my-tree',
  data: [
    { name: 'src', type: 'folder', children: [
      { name: 'index.html', type: 'file', url: 'src/index.html' },
      { name: 'styles.css', type: 'file', url: 'src/styles.css' }
    ]},
    { name: 'README.md', type: 'file', url: 'README.md' }
  ],

  // Optional UI hooks — pass selectors and ToolskinTree wires them up.
  search:   '#filter-input',
  expand:   '#expand-all',
  collapse: '#collapse-all',
  density:  { button: '#density-btn', target: '#tree-wrapper' },
  stats:    '#stats',     rootPath: '#root-path',     empty: '#empty'
});
</script>
